title: Iran’s largest crypto exchange founded by sons of family tied to supreme leaders: Reuters
summary: 1. Nobitex, Iran’s largest crypto exchange, was founded by brothers linked to the powerful Kharrazi family, which has ties to the country’s supreme leaders.
author: Cointelegraph by Amin Haqshanas

title: Poll: Americans uneasy with AI, crypto even as they spend big on midterms
summary: 1. An April POLITICO Poll finds broad skepticism about both crypto and AI, creating a growing tension for candidates benefiting from the influx of cash.
author: By Erin Doherty, Jasper Goodman, Jessica Piper, Daniel Barnes and Brendan Bordelon

title: France Scraps Dangerous Reporting Rule, Pension Fund Buys MSTR, And More – Week In Review
summary:
1. This week’s crypto stories spanned enforcement, mining, regulation, adoption, and institutional flows.
2. Chainalysis traced Iran-linked stablecoin routing behind a major USDT freeze, while Riot continued its steady BTC sales to NYDIG.
3. France dropped a proposed self-custody reporting rule, Binance argued crypto’s next wave of users will come through payments and utility, and Canada’s AIMCo disclosed […]
author: Alex Richardson

title: Latam Insights: Brazil Bans Crypto Transfers as Meta Launches USDC Payouts
summary:
1. Welcome to Latam Insights, a compilation of the most relevant crypto news from Latin America over the past week.
2. In this edition, Brazil’s central bank issues a resolution banning the use of crypto rails in regulated cross-border payments, Bitso estimates 40% of all crypto purchases include stablecoins, and Meta launches USDC payments in Colombia.
author: Sergio Goschenko

title: New York forces Uphold to pay $5M over fraudulent crypto investment scheme
summary: 1. New York AG Letitia James secured a $5 million settlement from Uphold for promoting CredEarn, a crypto savings product that misled users about its risks.
author: Cointelegraph by Amin Haqshanas

title: Worker Wins Landmark AI Substitution Case in China
summary:
1. Experts stated that the decision, which sets a precedent for future AI substitution cases, establishes that AI replacement cannot be brandished as a justification to abandon a labor contract.
2. China is the world’s second-largest AI hub.
3. Key Takeaways: The Hangzhou Court ruled that replacing Zhou with an AI model didn’t justify his forced termination.
author: Sergio Goschenko
